conclusion: The large-effect conditional-coverage gap is an ATTAINABILITY (information) limit, not a method deficiency. Under donor-blocked LODO on the committed pipeline, the best deployable method (CQR) reaches 0.744 top-stratum coverage while the true-magnitude oracle reaches 0.865 (nominal 0.90); the 0.121 [95% CI 0.107-0.136] residual gap cannot be closed by any label-free difficulty estimator because admissible features explain ~1.3% of |residual| variance versus ~97.5% explained by the true effect magnitude. This exhausts the localized/normalized/Mondrian conformal family (Papadopoulos 2008; Lei 2018; Guan 2023; Vovk 2003/2012) and is bounded by Barber-Candes-Ramdas-Tibshirani (2019/2021).

decision_rule_evaluation.honest_nuance: 5 of 6 deployable scores sit statistically above the 0.20 chance floor by ~0.01-0.03 (tight bootstrap CIs), i.e. a DETECTABLE but PRACTICALLY NEGLIGIBLE signal: max deployable capture 0.233 vs oracle 0.594; feature R2=0.013. This does NOT close the attainability gap and is reported faithfully rather than rounded to exactly-chance.

honesty_contract: NEGATIVE/LIMIT result reported as an honest null; committed pipeline scored (not surrogate); no post-hoc metric tuning; timestamp excluded from receipt hash per commit_gate convention.

information_limit_diagnostics.interpretation: Admissible features carry ~1.3% of the residual-magnitude information the true effect carries. The difficulty axis IS the label.

pipeline.split: donor-blocked LODO (4 folds); folds from data/interim/lodo_surface_cache.pkl

prior_art_exhausted: Papadopoulos, Gammerman, Vovk (2008) normalized nonconformity measures
Lei, G'Sell, Rinaldo, Tibshirani, Wasserman (2018, JASA) locally-weighted/normalized CP
Vovk et al. (2003), Vovk (2012) Mondrian / conditional-validity CP
Barber, Candes, Ramdas, Tibshirani (2019/2021) limits of distribution-free conditional coverage
title: Conditional-coverage ATTAINABILITY certificate (Frontier 1 negative result)
